label: Initial System & User Enum
command:
echo -e "
=== Identity ==="; id; echo -e "
=== System Info ==="; uname -a; cat /etc/os-release | grep -E "^PRETTY_NAME="; hostname; echo -e "
=== Activity ==="; w; last -n 5
description:
When: You land a shell and want comprehensive host and user context.

Output: Shows UID, kernel version, OS release, hostname, and recent logins.

Next: Check for special groups (sudo/docker) or kernel exploits.

label: Local Network Reconnaissance
command:
echo -e "
=== Interfaces ==="; ip a; echo -e "
=== Listening Ports ==="; ss -tulpn; echo -e "
=== Hosts File ==="; cat /etc/hosts
description:
When: Mapping the target's internal network exposure.

Output: Shows IP subnets, listening localhost ports, and hardcoded hostnames.

Next: Find services listening on 127.0.0.1 for port-forwarding.

label: SUID, SGID & Capabilities
command:
echo -e "
=== SUID Binaries ==="; find / -perm -4000 -type f -exec ls -la {} \; 2>/dev/null; echo -e "
=== SGID Binaries ==="; find / -perm -2000 -type f -exec ls -la {} \; 2>/dev/null; echo -e "
=== Capabilities ==="; getcap -r / 2>/dev/null
description:
When: Hunting for binaries executing with elevated privileges or special capabilities.

Output: Lists SUID/SGID files and capability assignments.

Next: Check GTFOBins for any custom or standard binaries found.

label: Scheduled Tasks & Timers
command:
echo -e "
=== User Crontab ==="; crontab -l; echo -e "
=== System Cron Dirs ==="; ls -la /etc/cron* 2>/dev/null; echo -e "
=== Systemd Timers ==="; systemctl list-timers --all
description:
When: Checking all scheduled task mechanisms for hijackable execution paths.

Output: Shows user crons, system crons, and active timers.

Next: Look for custom scripts running as root that are writable by your user.

label: Quick Config & File Hunting
command:
echo -e "
=== Writable Dirs ==="; find / -type d -writable 2>/dev/null | grep -v "/proc/"; echo -e "
=== SSH Keys ==="; find / -name id_rsa 2>/dev/null; echo -e "
=== Root Bash History ==="; cat /root/.bash_history 2>/dev/null; echo -e "
=== Config Passwords ==="; grep -Rn -i 'password\|passw\|pwd' /etc/ 2>/dev/null | head -n 20
description:
When: Hunting for low-hanging fruit: passwords, private keys, and writable drop-zones.

Output: Shows /tmp paths, id_rsa locations, command history, and credential hits.

Next: Use found credentials or SSH keys to pivot users.

label: Docker & Container Checks
command: echo -e "\n=== Container Env ==="; ls -la /.dockerenv 2>/dev/null; echo -e "\n=== Docker Socket ==="; find / -name docker.sock 2>/dev/null
description:
When: Determining containerization status and docker access.

Output: Reveals .dockerenv and readable docker sockets.

Next: Attempt container escapes or privileged docker spawning.

label: Nmap Target Initial Scan
description:
When: You need to identify open ports and services on a single Linux target before privilege escalation.

Output: Shows open ports, service versions, and default script output.

Next: Review services for known exploits or misconfigurations.

label: Nmap Full Port Scan
description:
When: You want to ensure no high or non-standard ports were missed.

Output: Lists all 65535 ports.

Next: Target any newly discovered ports for version scanning.

label: Read /etc/passwd
description:
When: Listing all local accounts and identifying shell paths.

Output: Shows users, home directories, and login shells.

Next: Identify users with /bin/bash who might have interactive logins.

label: Check Sudo Permissions
description:
When: Checking if the current user can run any commands as root without a password.

Output: Shows NOPASSWD entries or ALL=(ALL:ALL).

Next: Look up allowed binaries on GTFOBins.

label: Run pspy (Process Snooping)
description:
When: Monitoring running processes and cron jobs dynamically without root access.

Output: Shows processes launching, UID, and command line arguments in real-time.

Next: Identify hidden cron jobs or passwords passed passing via CLI.

label: Run LinPEAS
description:
When: Running the ultimate automated privilege escalation checker.

Output: Outputs color-coded findings highlighting 99% guaranteed privesc vectors.

Next: Investigate RED/YELLOW text immediately.

label: Run Linux Exploit Suggester
description:
When: Automatically matching kernel version against known exploits.

Output: Lists CVEs like PwnKit, DirtyCOW, etc.

Next: Download the exploit code and compile on the target.

label: GTFOBins: Python Sudo Shell
description:
When: Python is allowed in sudo -l without a password.

Output: Spawns a root shell.

Next: Use root access to capture flags or dump hashes.

label: GTFOBins: Vim Sudo Shell
description:
When: Vim is allowed in sudo -l without a password.

Output: Spawns a root shell from within vim.

Next: Use root access to capture flags or dump hashes.

label: GTFOBins: Find Sudo Shell
description:
When: Find is allowed in sudo -l without a password.

Output: Spawns a root shell.

Next: Use root access to capture flags or dump hashes.

label: GTFOBins: Awk Sudo Shell
description:
When: Awk is allowed in sudo -l without a password.

Output: Spawns a root shell.

Next: Use root access to capture flags or dump hashes.

label: GTFOBins: Nmap Sudo Shell
description:
When: Nmap is allowed in sudo -l without a password.

Output: Spawns a root shell by running a custom nse script.

Next: Use root access to capture flags or dump hashes.

label: GTFOBins: Less Sudo Shell
description:
When: Less is allowed in sudo -l without a password. Type !/bin/sh inside the pager.

Output: Spawns a root shell.

Next: Use root access to capture flags.

label: Tar Wildcard PrevEsc
description:
When: Tar is running blindly over a directory (e.g. tar *). Create files that look like tar flags.

Output: When cron runs tar *, it executes your script.

Next: Provide a payload script.sh to achieve root.

label: Stabilize Shell (Python PTY)
description:
When: You caught a reverse shell and need interactive features (Ctrl+C, TTY).

Output: Upgrades a basic netcat shell to a PTY.

Next: Export TERM and stty raw -echo for full stability.

label: NFS Root Squashing Check
description:
When: You want to check if the target has an exported NFS share that allows clients to run as root.

Output: Outputs the export line if no_root_squash is enabled.

Next: Mount the share locally, plant a SUID bash binary, and run it on the target.

label: Sudo LD_PRELOAD Hijacking
description:
When: Checking if sudo preserves the LD_PRELOAD environment variable when executing commands.

Output: Outputs the env_keep line if LD_PRELOAD is present.

Next: Compile a C shared library hijacking '_init', and run a permitted sudo command with LD_PRELOAD to spawn a root shell.

label: Writable /etc/passwd (Add Root User)
description:
When: You have write access to /etc/passwd.

Output: Appends a new user 'hacker' with password 'hacker' and root UID (0).

Next: Run 'su hacker' to switch to your new root account.

label: LXC/LXD Group Privilege Escalation
command: id | grep lxd && lxc image import ./alpine.tar.gz --alias myimage && lxc init myimage ignite -c security.privileged=true && lxc config device add ignite mydevice disk source=/ path=/mnt/root recursive=true && lxc start ignite && lxc exec ignite /bin/sh
description:
When: The current user is part of the 'lxd' or 'lxc' group.

Output: Spawns a root shell nested inside the container with the host's root filesystem mounted at /mnt/root.

Next: You are root on the host filesystem via /mnt/root.

label: SUID Shared Object Injection
description:
When: You found a custom SUID binary and want to see if it tries to load missing shared libraries (.so).

Output: Shows 'No such file or directory' errors for missing library paths.

Next: If the missing library path is writable by you, compile a malicious C library and place it there.

label: Dump Passwords from Memory
description:
When: You have read access to /dev/mem or /dev/kmem and want to hunt for plaintext credentials.

Output: Outputs strings matching 'PASS' found in live system memory.

Next: Look for SSH or database passwords to pivot users.

label: Locate Target for DirtyPipe
description:
When: Kernel is >= 5.8 and < 5.16.11, and you need to locate a target SUID file to overwrite with the DirtyPipe exploit.

Output: Outputs standard SUID binaries like /bin/su or /usr/bin/passwd.

Next: Execute the compiled DirtyPipe exploit against the discovered path.

label: Automated Exploit: PwnKit Check
command: dpkg -l | grep policykit-1 || rpm -qa | grep polkit
description:
When: Checking if Polkit (specifically pkexec) is installed and active on the system.

Output: Outputs installed versions of the polkit package.

Next: Download and compile the CVE-2021-4034 (PwnKit) exploit to gain an immediate root shell.
